rulings.Case Title: SIMA ZOKAEI VS. SALEM MANSOIR ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 24, 2023 line 1. RESPONDENT SALEM MANSOIR Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award is DENIED. Judgment entered in this action on March 3, 2023. Previous petition seeking to vacate this arbitration award, inter alia, as to the Association, (January 13, 2023 Petition 2:2-6; 10-11-12) was denied on February 23, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: YUTING WEN VS. JUN MIN ZHANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

rulings.Case Title: LORAN SIMON VS. TODD BRABEC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 24, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT 426 FILLMORE ASSOCATION DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. The Demurrer is sustained as to the: (1) 1st and 2nd causes of action with leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions, as to the elements of the claims; (2) 6th cause of action with leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions, as to the existence of a contract; (3) 16th cause of action with leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions, underlying both the intent and the nature of the economic relationships involved; and (4) 17th cause of action with leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions, as to the hostility element. The Demurrer is overruled for the 3rd and 8th causes of actions, as they have been sufficiently plead.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 57 TAYLOR I7, LP VS. FRANCISCO JUARES HERNANDEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 24, 2023 line 7. DEFENDANT FRANCISCO HERNANDEZ MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is OFF CALENDAR, insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and 1013. Motion filed and served on March 7, 2023, by mail for a hearing on March 24, 2023.
